item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the Latin in the article isn't really correct:

Est tempus nunc! Et Peregrinus incipit! Vivat Rex!

[…] Translation of the phrases spoken at the end produce the lines 'The time is now. The Crusade has begun. Long live the king.'

Alright, Est tempus nunc does mean "Now is the time", and Vivat Rex means "The King shall live."

But I think Peregrinus incepit would mean something like "The foreigner has begun." Crusade would probably be more similar to "Expeditio" or "Peregrinatio". I'd suggest "Nunc tempus est, peregrinatio incepto." or something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see why this would require the formatting difference it has, especially since in some aspects it makes it a bit harder to understand what it does. I think it would actually be better if it was the normal scp format. Other than that, I really like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted specifically because of the phrase "King Data the Expunged of Site Redacted".

That is a brilliant phrase. +1

item.title: 我想去死，I want to die
item.content:encoded:
这篇文章好难翻译，我一星期都没有翻译完
This article is difficult to translate, I haven't translated for a week
但我很喜欢这篇文章
But I love this article

item.title: Royal We
item.content:encoded: I know this is a bit of an opinion, but I feel like the last note would read better and more thematically in line if first person was replaced by the royal we

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: No worries. Thanks for asking about it!

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Was not aware of that, so fair enough. It's a minor thing anyhow, I just noticed and thought it was odd that an apparently grammatical correction was reverted. Thanks for the clarification!

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded:
I think the reason for the revert wasn't due to the amnestic/amnesiac change; it was because the edit was made without permission.

Given that this article was posted a few years ago and some authors prefer to use "amnesiac" rather than "amnestic" to keep in style with the articles posted at the beginning of the site's history, it's been decided to let "amnesiac" remain as a stylistic choice.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded:
I'd motion to change it back to 'amnestic' again, as that refers to something that causes amnesia while 'amnesiac' refers to someone that suffers from amnesia. Given the context it is pretty clear that the former is intended.

I'd make this change myself as per the 'small edits for spelling and grammar' rule, but since this exact change has been reverted by staff before I thought I'd better ask the staff member who reverted the change in the first place to make the call instead.

item.title: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Version 24 of this article was reverted, as it changed 'amnesiac' to 'amnestic' without noted permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because it is impossible to describe this without being affected, from the tag glossary:

infohazard: SCP has an effect that is triggered when it is described or referred to.

Memes are information and therefore the terms aren't mutually exclusive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this skip tagged infohazard when the article itself — both the document provided by the anomalous kingdom and the Foundation addenda — says that it's a memetic hazard?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: one of the best i have read, if not THE best. +1 10/10

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is beautiful. I noticed a small grammatical error:

He refused to speak to any personnel struggled against his restraints

But I'm afraid to attempt to fix it, for fear of being reprimanded by staff. ^^;

Definitely one of my favorites, though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did I not see this? Why?

+1 This is one of the best SCPs I have ever seen

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I give it a +1 because I like the peace treaty at the end. Good job and long live the king!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Surprised I haven't seen this one before. I'm feeling like this is too silly for mainlist, despite the serious points, but ultimately I'm conflicted, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Verily, my heartiest upvotes are offered at the throne of the Most Glorious King Data the Expunged. May his forthcoming alliance with the Kingdom of the Council bring peace and prosperity to their peoples. Long live the King!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Most Glorious King Data the Expunged, Ruler of the Kingdom of Site Redacted

I have no words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A CK in the making. Why didn't they nuke the site?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that it's not the "Destroy Destroy Destroy Foundation", but still, I'd think that a mind control SCP this powerful would be destroyed. Or at least encased in a huge concrete cube with strict orders to leave it alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Though it doesn't sit well with me that the Foundation can't take back their site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, all the king's subjects are Protestant, which would probably piss off King Eugenio.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone which can pull off midieval-style writing in their SCP this well deserves major kudos. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the bunny Roman Catholic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just read it now, and they are really similar.

I'm actually thinking of revising this document to better fit that story, if you'd let me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THEEEE PERFFFECCCCTTTT THROWWWW

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm torn on whether I want to upvote this or downvote it. I've always been a fan of format screw, and I like what this is going for, but I'm not sure how I feel about the implementation. Document 1561-1 has so many [UNINTELLIGIBLE]s that it's nigh-unreadable, for one. Also, since there's nothing anomalous or infohazardous about the pseudo-SCP casefile itself (since Dr. ██ was able to add a note at the end), shouldn't there be some sort of note at the top saying something to the effect of "this SCP object is involved in an ongoing containment breach, please bear with us as this is the closest to an official casefile we can give you right now". Otherwise, anyone who needs access to containment protocols is just gonna be handed this document full of Ye Olde Englishe and expected to figure it out on their own. For that matter, why hasn't the Foundation used this document to write up their own temporary casefile about the crown's effects (known and presumed) and how to re-establish containment of it?

Anyway like I said, I'm on the fence about how I want to vote here. I'll read it over again in a few days before I decide. Right now though, I'm leaning towards a downvote.

(Also, I noticed two occasions (one in the Royal Containment Decree and another in Royal Decree MDLXI-I) where the king was referred to as Data the Redacted instead of Data the Expunged. Was that intentional?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, yeah, but having to cover up the nuke (and handling containment…issues that might arise) would probably be easier than dealing with a competent megalomaniac imbued with mind-bending powers.
Unless they foolhardily built Site [REDACTED] in vicinity of some inhabited place, which would be Umbrella Corporation stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. People would notice
  2. There are SCPs in there

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh, I smiled.

I think I might upvote.

Question:
why don't they don't just, you know, nuke the site from orbit ? ;-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Most Glorious King Data the Expunged, Ruler of the Kingdom of Site Redacted

At the very least you establish that it is meant as a parody, so I won't downvote, which I was initially going to. Though I will keep my upvote for the person who makes a scip that actually translates the article into Old English.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I spotted the verb-subject agreement problem as well and decided against changing it. I'd say leave it in as an in-joke-after all, this thing just makes people believe they're in a renfair universe-it doesn't actually teach them archaic English (as far as I can see in the article, at least.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yup, it's very similar, though IMO you did it better, Dmatix.

With a bit of adapting, though, this SCP could easily reach that territory. Maybe even link them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hold a second. This is, almost word for word, the unnumbered SCP that the tale Guard Duty is about. The interview log is also highly reminiscent of the end of that particular tale (other than that the guard in question got better, and this one here didn't).

E: Just so it's clear, this isn't me blaming you of anything, it's just quite odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The changes you've made are positive, and the SCP section kinda works. It's as if Site-[REDACTED] insists on having the file that way. I'm glad you stuck by your ideas and removed the Athur references. The addenda work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IN THE TREE, PART OF THE TREE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mmm, the basic idea is OK, but do not attempt to use Mediaeval sounding English if you can't do it correctly. Case in point: -t is second person singular ending. 'Men… art' and 'commoners… shalt' is wrong. Fix this and the rest of the wonky language in the first part (I would strongly suggest you don't try to make it sound old, but rather just very formal. Consider looking at some recent royal decrees online) and I'll remove my downvote.

To get an upvote, you'd have to make it less… silly. I know silly isn't necessarily bad, but attaching a note to a rabbit seems a bit lolsorandum when it would surely make more sense for His Majesty to send an ambassador/messenger - a human, anyway.

That said, the coremost idea of an SCP that makes a site rebel is sound, and I don't object too strongly to the whole regal angle. I also had a little giggle at 'His Majesty Data the Expunged'. There's definitely something here, but it needs more work yet.

E: regarding the Latin phrase, it's correct, but it would sound more Latin if it was 'tempus nunc est. peregrinus incipit. vivat rex!' - there tended to be an inclination towards putting less important verbs towards the end of sentences. Also, you've included the word 'et' without translating it.

EE: on re-reading, it's actually not too bad at all. I've removed my downvote, and I'm hopeful for a slight tightening of seriousness. I think the problem I'm having with it is that in the best silly articles (Larry the Llama, Squeedle Dee Dee, me, etc) the silliness gives way to a sense of horror. There is definitely a big threat from this SCP - namely, you can't trust the guards, who are what are keeping us all safe - but I don't feel it comes across quite strongly enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly my same reason to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is he really like the Hanged King? I didn't get that impression at all. I figured he was just some D-Class or Chaos Insurgent who jacked the crown and took over the Site.

I really like this idea, but I definitely think it needs a rewrite. It feels very sloppy right now, but with more planning, figuring out the best way to format the article in order to tell the story, I think it would be pretty great.

Hey, in the interview logs, would the name of the item still come out as His Majesty's SCP MDLXI? I mean, it's being spoken aloud. We don't say Queen Elizabeth Aye-Aye. A straight transcription of the brothers' conversation seem like it should be "His Majesty's SCP The One Thousand Five Hundred And First" or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs a rewrite, so I'm downvoting to encourage one. The King of Data Expunged is the most interesting part of the article, so I'd talk less about him. Yes, he's essentially the Hanged King, but you can do something much more interesting with less information than you can with what you have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meant to insinuate something about who was doing the log, but I don't think I did it too clearly (see above posts).

Changed that part to it's translation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it except for the part when someone called clear Latin gibberish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Crown makes them royal, the wearer himself is insignificant. But I see your point, I'll think on that. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see a good idea here, but I'm just not sure how I feel about it.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made a few changes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor tonal thing: wouldn't it make more sense to talk about the chosen one who puts the crown upon his head and becomes King Data the Expunged, rather than the 'unworthy person'? This is a report by people driven mad to believe he is king, after all…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It takes on the name of whatever people call it and 'corrects' it to fit an old English theme.

So whomever had it last, if he called it the 'kick-ass crown' it would be corrected to 'The Regal Crown of Arse-punts'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Way too silly for my tastes. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love it! One question though….

Mere knowledge of His Majesty's SCP-MDLXI shalt cause a compulsion to refer to The Royal Crown justly as his Majesty's SCP-MDLXI.

Did people in ye old yonder yesteryear also call it 'His Majesty's SCP-MDLXI? Or did it have some alternate title before it was in Foundation custody?

Just a little thought though. Other than that, love it! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "One of US, One of US, ONE OF US.."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THE CROWN COMPELS YOU TO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like these kinds of SCPs. I don't know why, but I do. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stuff similar to it's been done before, but I quite like this. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I saw some minor typos, but this is actually a pretty novel idea. Get it cleaned up and I can up vote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
◾SCP-1561 - [ACCESS DENIED]

Ya forgot to change the title. :I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or maybe you can find a way to incorporate that even though they've all been infected, they are still running the site at its most basic level (securing, containing, protecting and writing reports). You could then explain that this report was recovered from them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know it wasn't gibberish, but someone on the inside was badly trying to convince everyone else who wasn't in on the plan that the phrases meant nothing, or at least delay someone catching on that there was a reason the guard spoke directly at the camera.

Sorry if I mucked up Latin a bit.
